questions.q: The famous Hindi cinema screenwriting duo Salim-Javed was made up of Salim Khan and which other writer?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed was the partnership of Salim Khan and Javed Akhtar, who together wrote many of Hindi cinema's biggest 1970s hits.

questions.q: Salim-Javed are most celebrated for pioneering which screen archetype in 1970s Hindi cinema?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed created the 'angry young man' template, embodied most famously by Amitabh Bachchan.

questions.q: Which 1975 multi-starrer 'curry western' was written by Salim-Javed and is often called one of the greatest Indian films ever made?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed wrote Sholay (1975), the legendary action drama directed by Ramesh Sippy.

questions.q: The 1973 action film Zanjeer, written by Salim-Javed, launched which actor's 'angry young man' image?
questions.explanation: Zanjeer (1973) gave Amitabh Bachchan his breakthrough as the brooding, intense inspector Vijay.

questions.q: Which member of the Salim-Javed duo is the father of filmmaker Farhan Akhtar and director Zoya Akhtar?
questions.explanation: Javed Akhtar is the father of Farhan Akhtar and Zoya Akhtar, both prominent filmmakers.

questions.q: Salim Khan of Salim-Javed is the father of which leading Bollywood superstar?
questions.explanation: Salim Khan is the father of actor Salman Khan, along with Arbaaz and Sohail Khan.

questions.q: In the 1975 Salim-Javed film Deewaar, the line 'Mere paas maa hai' is spoken in a confrontation between two on-screen what?
questions.explanation: In Deewaar, the iconic 'Mere paas maa hai' is delivered by Shashi Kapoor's character to his brother, played by Amitabh Bachchan.

questions.q: Salim-Javed wrote the original 1978 version of which action thriller featuring a double role for Amitabh Bachchan?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed wrote Don (1978), in which Amitabh Bachchan played both the gangster Don and his lookalike Vijay.

questions.q: Salim Khan, before becoming a screenwriter, briefly worked in Hindi cinema in which role?
questions.explanation: Salim Khan began his career as an actor in Hindi films before turning to screenwriting with Javed Akhtar.

questions.q: Which character name did Salim-Javed repeatedly give to Amitabh Bachchan's heroes in films like Zanjeer, Deewaar and Sholay?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed frequently named Amitabh Bachchan's characters 'Vijay', which became a signature of their writing.

questions.q: According to the deck, in which year did the screenwriting partnership of Salim-Javed begin its active run?
questions.explanation: The Salim-Javed partnership was active from 1971 to 1982, producing a string of landmark Hindi films.

questions.q: Salim-Javed are noted as being among the first Indian screenwriters to receive what kind of public recognition?
options: A National Film Award for direction
A share of overseas distribution rights
Star billing on film posters and credits
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed were among the first Indian screenwriters to demand and receive star billing for their writing.

questions.q: In which year was the Salim-Javed-written blockbuster Sholay released?
questions.explanation: Sholay was released in 1975 and went on to become one of the longest-running films in Indian cinema history.

questions.q: Which menacing dacoit villain did Salim-Javed create for their 1975 film Sholay?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed wrote the iconic villain Gabbar Singh for Sholay, memorably played by Amjad Khan.

questions.q: Salim-Javed wrote Zanjeer (1973), which was directed by which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Zanjeer was directed by Prakash Mehra from a screenplay by Salim-Javed.

questions.q: The Salim-Javed films Deewaar (1975), Trishul (1978) and Kaala Patthar (1979) were all directed by which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Yash Chopra directed Deewaar, Trishul and Kaala Patthar, all written by Salim-Javed.

questions.q: In the Salim-Javed-written Sholay, what is the name of the wisecracking small-time crook played by Dharmendra?
questions.explanation: Dharmendra played Veeru, one of the two mercenaries hired by Thakur in Sholay.

questions.q: Which 1980 Salim-Javed film, directed by Ramesh Sippy, was a big-budget ensemble action adventure released after Sholay?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed wrote Shaan (1980), Ramesh Sippy's lavish action film featuring villain Shakaal.

questions.q: The 1973 film Yaadon Ki Baaraat, an early Salim-Javed screenplay, is often cited as a template for which type of Hindi film plot?
questions.explanation: Yaadon Ki Baaraat helped popularize the 'brothers separated in childhood and reunited' masala formula in Hindi cinema.

questions.q: After Salim-Javed split as a writing team, which member went on to become a celebrated lyricist and poet?
questions.explanation: After the split, Javed Akhtar became a renowned lyricist and Urdu poet, winning multiple awards for his songs.

questions.q: Which 1977 Manmohan Desai film with a 'lost and found' plot was written by Salim-Javed and became a major hit for Amitabh Bachchan?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed wrote Amar Akbar Anthony (1977), Manmohan Desai's hugely successful tale of three separated brothers.

questions.q: According to the deck, in which year did the Salim-Javed screenwriting partnership end?
questions.explanation: The Salim-Javed partnership was active until 1982, after which the two writers worked separately.

questions.q: Javed Akhtar of Salim-Javed was honoured with which of India's highest civilian awards in 2007?
questions.explanation: Javed Akhtar received the Padma Shri in 1999 and the Padma Bhushan in 2007 for his contribution to the arts.

questions.q: Salim-Javed wrote the screenplay for which 1972 Ramesh Sippy double-role comedy-drama starring Hema Malini in a twin role?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed wrote Seeta Aur Geeta (1972), Ramesh Sippy's hit in which Hema Malini played twin sisters separated at birth.

questions.q: Which 1974 crime film, an early hit for Salim-Javed, was directed by Prakash Mehra and starred Vinod Khanna and Randhir Kapoor?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed wrote Haath Ki Safai (1974), the Prakash Mehra caper that won a Filmfare Best Story award.

questions.q: In the Salim-Javed-written Sholay, which veteran actor played the armless ex-policeman Thakur Baldev Singh?
questions.explanation: Sanjeev Kumar played Thakur Baldev Singh, who hires Jai and Veeru to capture Gabbar Singh.

questions.q: In Salim-Javed's 1978 film Trishul, directed by Yash Chopra, which actor played the illegitimate son Vijay who sets out to ruin his estranged father's business?
questions.explanation: In Trishul (1978), Amitabh Bachchan played Vijay, who builds a rival empire to destroy the father (Sanjeev Kumar) who abandoned his mother.

questions.q: Which 1974 Salim-Javed film starring Amitabh Bachchan was an adaptation in which the hero, wrongly believing he is dying, frames himself for a murder?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed wrote Majboor (1974), in which Amitabh Bachchan's character confesses to a crime he did not commit.

questions.q: Salim Khan and Javed Akhtar were jointly honoured with a Lifetime Achievement-style recognition by which awards body in 2013 for their contribution to screenwriting?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed received the Filmfare Lifetime Achievement Award in 2013, recognizing their landmark screenwriting partnership.

questions.q: The two mercenaries Jai and Veeru, and the dialogue-driven 'masala' action style, marked Salim-Javed's biggest cultural impact during which decade of Hindi cinema?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed defined the 1970s in Hindi cinema, shaping the masala film and the angry young man era.
